common.errorRetry: Check your connection settings and try again.

queue.title: Waiting for you
queue.subtitle: Nothing is written to Jira, Xray or Git without your approval.
queue.checkFailed: Couldn't check what's waiting for you right now.
queue.blocking_one: {{count}} blocking finding to resolve before release
queue.blocking_other: {{count}} blocking findings to resolve before release
queue.trains_one: {{count}} fix train held for your confirmation
queue.trains_other: {{count}} fix trains held for your confirmation

precision.summary: Engineers reviewed {{reviewed}} findings and ruled on {{decided}} — {{pct}}% kept (accepted or sent to Jira) · {{jira}} sent to Jira.
precision.summaryNoRate: Engineers reviewed {{reviewed}} findings — {{jira}} sent to Jira.
precision.disputed: The AI disputed {{count}} of its own findings first.

errors.copilotAuth: Sign in to Copilot to run AI analysis — open Settings to connect.
errors.network: Can't reach the Veritas server. Check that it's running, then try again.
errors.denied: Access was denied. Check the connection credentials in Settings.
errors.notFound: We couldn't find that — it may have been removed or renamed.
errors.timeout: That request took too long and timed out. Try again in a moment.
errors.server: The server hit a problem finishing that. Try again shortly — if it keeps happening, check the server logs.

snyk.title: Snyk
snyk.subtitle: Dependency security — watch your repositories and act on vulnerabilities.

impact.title: Dependency security
impact.foundSub: {{open}} open · {{fixable}} with a safe version available

snyk.connectHint: Connect Snyk in Settings to see your organizations.
snyk.connectBody: Veritas reads your apps’ vulnerabilities from Snyk. Connect your account once — it takes about a minute.
snyk.connectStep1: Create a personal API token in your Snyk account at
snyk.connectStep2: Open Settings → Snyk, paste the token, and click Save.
snyk.connectStep3: Click Test connection. When it turns green, your Snyk applications appear here to watch.
snyk.connectFailed: Couldn’t connect to Snyk — check the token (and the region/base URL in Settings).
snyk.connectAdvanced: On a different Snyk region, or need advanced options?
snyk.openSettings: Check your token in Settings → Snyk
snyk.tokenRequiredBody: Veritas needs your Snyk API token to read your applications. Add it once in Settings — it takes about a minute.
snyk.tokenRequiredHint: Add your Snyk API token in Settings to load this.
snyk.addBody: Select the applications to watch — Veritas tracks each one’s application-tests repository.
snyk.noApps: No applications found for your Snyk token.
snyk.watchHint: Each selected application is watched via its application-tests repository.
snyk.watchSomeFailed_one: {{count}} application couldn’t be watched (no application-tests repo).
snyk.watchSomeFailed_other: {{count}} applications couldn’t be watched (no application-tests repo).
snyk.noWatchesBody: Add a repository above to track its Snyk vulnerabilities and get notified when new ones appear.
snyk.issuesEmpty: No vulnerabilities — this repository is clean.
snyk.tipCve: CVE — the public identifier for this vulnerability.
snyk.tipCvss: CVSS — severity score from 0 to 10 (higher is worse).
snyk.tipFixable: Whether Snyk has a safe version to upgrade to.

bulk.openHint: Pick the vulnerabilities to fix across your watched applications.
bulk.noneHint: Nothing to fix right now — your watched applications have no fixable vulnerabilities.
bulk.title: Fix vulnerabilities
bulk.loadFailed: Couldn’t load the fixable vulnerabilities. Try refreshing.
bulk.emptyBody: None of your watched applications has a vulnerability with a safe version available.

wizard.step4Title: File it in Jira, then start
wizard.connIntro: Before we start, let’s make sure Veritas can reach the tools it needs.
wizard.connLoadFailed: Couldn’t check your connections. Try again.
wizard.connAllGood: Everything Veritas needs is connected — you’re good to go.
wizard.connBlocked: Some required connections aren’t ready. Fix them in Settings, then come back.
wizard.connNeedReady: Connect the required tools before continuing.
wizard.pickIntro: Choose the vulnerabilities to fix across your watched applications.
wizard.pickLoadFailed: Couldn’t load the fixable vulnerabilities. Try refreshing.
wizard.pickEmptyBody: None of your watched applications has a vulnerability with a safe version available.
wizard.needSelection: Pick at least one vulnerability to continue.
wizard.reviewIntro: Here’s what Veritas will do when you start.
wizard.reviewFramework: First, we’ll upgrade the shared test framework (APP7488).
wizard.reviewApps: Then we’ll open pull requests for these applications:
wizard.reviewSafety: Each upgrade is build-verified locally; anything that breaks is held for your review — Veritas never merges on its own.
wizard.projectHint: Every ticket for this batch is filed here.
wizard.projectLoadFailed: Couldn’t load your Jira projects. Try again.
wizard.projectEmpty: No Jira projects were found for your account.
wizard.epicHint: Group this batch of fixes under one epic.
wizard.epicLoadFailed: Couldn’t load epics for this project.
wizard.epicNone: No epics yet — create a new one below.
wizard.epicSummaryHint: We’ll create this epic when you start.
wizard.storyHint: Every fix in this batch is tracked under one story inside the epic.
wizard.storyLoadFailed: Couldn’t load stories for this epic.
wizard.storyNone: No open stories in this epic — create a new one.
wizard.storySummaryHint: We’ll create this story under the epic when you start.
wizard.reviewersHint: Pick Bitbucket users to review every pull request in this batch.
wizard.needProject: Choose a Jira project to continue.
wizard.needEpic: Choose an existing epic, or create a new one.
wizard.needEpicSummary: Give the new epic a title.
wizard.needStory: Choose an existing open story, or create a new one.
wizard.needStorySummary: Give the new story a title.
wizard.started: Started {{fixes}} fixes across {{apps}} applications, filed under {{story}}.
wizard.startedSome: Filed under {{story}}. {{ok}} of {{total}} applications started; {{failed}} had a problem.

success.filed: Your fixes are filed in Jira and running.

fix.title: Fix {{pkg}} → {{version}}
fix.intro: This updates {{pkg}} from {{from}} to {{to}} across the shared test framework and the applications you select.
fix.introAside: Update order: framework modules first (BOM → core → api/web), then one pull request per application.
fix.appsHint: Each app's application-tests repo picks up the new version.
fix.jiraKeyHint: Use a ticket you already have.
fix.jiraProjectHint: Veritas creates the ticket with the fix details.
fix.reviewersHint: Comma-separated usernames; blank auto-suggests from git history.
fix.bitbucketNote: Veritas clones the framework repos, pushes the version-bump branches and opens the PRs through your Bitbucket connection. Add a Bitbucket access token in Settings → Bitbucket before you start — without it the branches push but the PRs can’t be opened for you.
fix.detailSubtitle: Live status of this dependency fix — clone, breaking-change check, build & test, and pull requests.

whatToDo.AWAITING_CONFIRM: We planned the update train — which repos change, in what order, and to which version. Review the versions and reviewers below, then Confirm & run to start the build.
whatToDo.AWAITING_MANUAL_FIX: This bump needs a human. Adapt the code/tests on the pushed branches, then let Veritas open the held PRs — or open them yourself and paste each PR URL below.
whatToDo.PR_OPEN: The pull requests are open and assigned. Nothing else runs until a human merges them in Bitbucket; once every PR is merged, click Mark all merged to close this out.

fix.reviewIntro: Review the update train before it runs. Framework repos (APP7488) merge first, then one PR per app. Edit each module’s new version or its reviewers; greyed rows can’t be auto-bumped.
fix.breakingHelp: Breaking change flagged — adapt the code on the pushed branches and test, then open the PRs (yourself, or via Veritas).
fix.verdictBreaking: AI check: breaking change likely ({{c}}% confidence)
fix.verdictClean: AI check: no breaking change expected ({{c}}% confidence)
fix.verdictNone: AI check: not available — the test build is the gate
fix.slowHint: This step can take a few minutes — still working, not stuck.
fix.slowHintLong: Still working ({{elapsed}}) — long AI checks and full builds are normal.

status.CANCELLED: Cancelled — the Jira ticket and any pushed branch were left as-is; relaunch to reuse them.
status.ALREADY_FIXED: Already on the safe version — nothing to release, no PR opened.

PLANNING.label: Getting the code
PLANNING.detail: Cloning the framework and your app from Bitbucket.

CHECKING.label: Checking for breaking changes
CHECKING.detail: The AI assesses whether the upgrade is safe to apply.

VERIFYING.label: Building & testing locally
VERIFYING.detail: Bumping BOM → core → api/web → app and running each app’s tests.

OPENING_PRS.label: Opening the pull requests
OPENING_PRS.detail: Pushing the version-bump branches and opening one PR per module.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: API accuracy, test coverage and cost across your services.
overview.setupBody: {{names}} need attention before Veritas can run.
overview.setupBodyMore: {{names}} and {{count}} more need attention before Veritas can run.
overview.kpiBreakingSub: drift that would have broken a live consumer

verdictHeadline.WARN: Some services need clean-up before shipping
verdictHeadline.FAIL: Some services must not be released

overview.heroHonesty_zero: No blind spots — the latest audits could see everything they needed.
overview.heroHonesty_one: {{count}} blind spot on the latest audits — a source the analysis couldn't read.
overview.heroHonesty_other: {{count}} blind spots on the latest audits — sources the analysis couldn't read.
overview.valueHoursTooltip: Assumption: one manual contract review ≈ {{h}} hours of a senior reviewer.
overview.pipelineSubtitle: Each service's latest grade, visibility and test assets — with links to its findings and strategy.
overview.noValidationsBody: Validate a service to see its findings and a management report here.

onboarding.pageSubtitle: Three quick steps and you can validate a contract, manage tests and generate automation — all from here.
onboarding.getSetUpSubtitle: Everything is configured in the app — no terminal, no env vars.
onboarding.step1Body: Veritas uses Copilot as its reasoning engine. Sign in once on the Settings page.
onboarding.step2Body: Add your Bitbucket base URL + token so Veritas can discover and clone your repos.
onboarding.step3Body: Optional, but needed for defects, test management and coverage. Test each connection live in Settings.
onboarding.readyBody: Run your first contract validation to see findings and an executive report.
onboarding.notReadyBody: Finish the steps above, then run your first validation.

glossary.pageSubtitle: What the words on the report and the findings list actually mean — in plain language.
glossary.intro: Veritas compares what a service's <1>code actually does</1> with what its <3>API contract promises</3>, and lists every difference as a <5>finding</5>. The terms below explain how those findings are labelled so you can judge them without reading the code.
glossary.bottomLineSubtitle: The one-glance verdict at the top of every management report.
glossary.proceedBody: The contract matches the code closely enough to release. No release-blocking differences were found.
glossary.holdBody: The service works, but the contract has gaps worth correcting before you publish it. Nothing here blocks a release on its own.
glossary.doNotReleaseBody: At least one release-blocking difference was found — something a consumer relies on is wrong or missing. Fix these before shipping.
glossary.severitySubtitle: How much a single difference matters. Higher severity = more impact on the people calling the API.
glossary.blockerBody: Will break callers in production. Must be fixed before release.
glossary.criticalBody: A serious mismatch that is very likely to cause failures or confusion. Fix before release.
glossary.majorBody: A real gap worth correcting — the contract is misleading, but most callers will still work.
glossary.minorBody: A small inaccuracy or polish item. Safe to defer.
glossary.infoBody: An observation, not a defect. Nothing to fix.
glossary.confidenceSubtitle: How sure Veritas is that a finding is real. Use it to decide what to double-check.
glossary.confidenceHighBody: Proven directly from the code or the contract. Safe to act on as-is.
glossary.confidenceMediumBody: Well-supported, but worth a quick look before raising a defect.
glossary.confidenceLowBody: A reasonable suspicion that needs a human to confirm. A <1>high-severity finding marked low confidence</1> is flagged with a warning icon — verify it before you treat it as a blocker.
glossary.analysisAreaSubtitle: Which part of the API a finding is about. Veritas checks six areas; you never need the internal codes.
glossary.layerBlurbL1: Is the contract itself well-formed and structurally valid?
glossary.layerBlurbL2: Does the contract list every endpoint the code actually exposes — and nothing it doesn't?
glossary.layerBlurbL3: Are the endpoints, parameters and responses documented clearly enough to use?
glossary.layerBlurbL4: Do the request and response shapes in the contract match what the code really sends and accepts?
glossary.layerBlurbL5: Does the API follow sound design conventions (naming, status codes, consistency)?
glossary.layerBlurbL6: Is there enough in the contract to derive a solid set of tests from it?
glossary.reviewStatusSubtitle: What you've decided about a finding. Your decision is recorded with your name and the time.
glossary.acceptedBody: A real difference that matches the code. It stays in the management report and can be raised as a defect.
glossary.rejectedBody: Not applicable to this service. It's removed from the report and won't be raised as a defect. You can change this later.
glossary.defectRaisedBody: A Jira defect has been created for this finding and is now tracked there.
glossary.reportMetricsSubtitle: The numbers on the management report.
glossary.contractFidelityBody: The release verdict from a severity-count gate: FAIL if any counted finding would break a running consumer, WARN if only additive/documentation drift remains, PASS when clean. Thresholds are configurable (veritas.gate.*).
glossary.analysisCoverageBody: How much of the service Veritas was able to examine. <1>Full</1> means every source it needed was available; <3>Partial</3> means something (a security source, an exception handler, a DTO) wasn't supplied, so some checks were skipped.
glossary.estAnalysisCostBody: The estimated cost of the AI calls used for this validation. Most checks are done without AI; it's used only where a deterministic check isn't enough.

costs.loadError: Couldn't load the AI spend breakdown.
costs.pageSubtitle: Estimated Copilot spend in this environment, broken down by skill.
costs.trendSubtitle: Estimated AI cost per day, last 30 days.
costs.emptyBody: Costs appear here after your first AI-powered run.

defects.title: Defects
defects.subtitle: Jira defects raised from contract findings, with live status.
defects.openOver7DaysHint: Aging — still open past a week
defects.emptyBody: Defects you raise from findings will appear here, and Veritas will keep their Jira status in sync.
defects.emptyFilterBody: No defects match this severity. Clear the filter to see all defects.

evolve.title: Engine Evolution
evolve.subtitle: Field-learned classifications the engine promotes into itself via a reviewed pull request.
evolve.debtUnspecified: unclassified — this loop drives it to zero
evolve.emptyBody: When a new finding type ships unclassified and reviewers classify it in the field, its proposal appears here.
evolve.overrideHint: overriding the AI — a reason is required
evolve.commentPlaceholder: Explain the override (goes in the PR)…
evolve.saveFirst: Save your change first, then open the PR.
evolve.dryRunHint: Render this edit to a local review folder — no clone, no PR (debug flag required).
evolve.disputesSubtitle: Findings the engine flagged, then disputed as likely false positives — grouped by type so you can see which comparison rules keep misfiring, and record whether each dispute was right.
evolve.disputesEmptyBody: When the reconcile step flags one of its own findings as a likely false positive, it appears here for triage.

gates.title: Approval gates
gates.subtitle: Before Veritas writes anything to Jira, Xray or Git, it asks you first. Approvals wait here.
gates.emptyBody: When a skill needs approval before writing to Jira, Xray or Git, it will appear here.

repos.pageSubtitle: Find a service by its app-id, then check whether its API spec matches the code.
repos.appIdHint: Your project key in Bitbucket (ask your admin if unsure), e.g. CIAM or APP7571. This is not the repository name.
repos.emptyFoundBody: No repositories matched that app-id, or your token cannot access them. Check the app-id and your Bitbucket token in Settings.
repos.emptySearchBody: Enter an app-id above to list the repositories your Bitbucket token can access.
repos.specRepoPathHint: Path to the OpenAPI/Swagger file, relative to the repo root.
repos.specLiveDocsHint: A running endpoint that serves the OpenAPI JSON.
repos.specConfluenceHint: Paste the page URL or just its numeric ID — the page must hold the OpenAPI/Swagger spec.
repos.modalIntroPrefix: Veritas reads the API endpoints and data types defined in
repos.modalIntroSuffix: and compares them to the spec below. Read-only — nothing in the repository is changed. This usually takes 2–5 minutes.
repos.copilotWarnPrefix: Not connected to GitHub Copilot — the
repos.copilotWarnSuffix: step will be skipped. You'll still get the full deterministic contract findings and the release-gate verdict (PASS/WARN/FAIL).
repos.thoroughnessHint: How deeply the AI reviews the findings — trades cost for rigour.
repos.thoroughnessDeep: Deep — strongest model, for a release gate
repos.aiHintShort: The AI is reviewing the findings and generating a corrected spec — usually a minute or two.
repos.aiHintMedium: Still generating the corrected spec — larger contracts take a few minutes.
repos.aiHintLong: This is a large contract — the AI is still working ({{elapsed}}). You can keep waiting or run it in the background.
repos.scanStoppedNote: The scan stopped before it finished. Nothing was written to the repo.
repos.scanStaticNote: Veritas runs entirely from static analysis — your app is never started. Keep watching, or run it in the background.
repos.adjustAndRetry: Adjust the inputs and try again

reviews.noDetail: No detail captured for this review.
reviews.pageSubtitle: Each test gets a score and suggested improvements.
reviews.newReviewSubtitle: Find tests by JQL, pick which to review, optionally write the verdict back to Jira.
reviews.jqlHint: Which Xray tests, e.g. project = CIAM AND issuetype = Test — load to pick a subset, or review all
reviews.toastNoCandidates: That search returned no Xray tests.
reviews.applyLabel: Apply the corrected steps back to Xray (gated write)
reviews.recentSubtitle: Previously scored tests — reopen a verdict any time.
reviews.emptyNoReviewBody: Run a review above to see per-test verdicts and scores.
reviews.emptyNoMatchBody: That search returned no Xray tests to review.

findings.title: Findings
findings.subtitle: Differences between the documented API and what the code actually does. Review each one, mark it Accepted or Rejected, then raise a Jira defect if it needs fixing.
findings.reportTooltip: Open the management report — findings, the accuracy score and recommendations, for managers and developers.
findings.downloadTooltip: Download the report as a self-contained HTML file — the accept/reject controls for manual-review items keep working offline.
findings.emptyBody: The code matches the documented API exactly. Open the management report for the details, or validate again with a different spec.
findings.riskyConfidenceTooltip: High severity but the engine is only low-confidence here — verify before acting.
findings.acceptTooltip: Accept — this is a real difference that matches the code
findings.rejectTooltip: Reject — not applicable to this service; it won't appear in reports
findings.rejectModalBody: Once rejected, this finding is excluded from the management report and won't be raised as a defect. It's recorded under your name and can be changed later.
findings.findingsGetBug_one: {{count}} finding will each get a Bug issue.
findings.findingsGetBug_other: {{count}} findings will each get a Bug issue.
findings.projectKeyHint: Where each Bug issue will be created, e.g. CIAM.

testStrategy.pageSubtitle: Veritas drafts the strategy — you review and approve.
testStrategy.newStrategySubtitle: Risk register, approach, exit criteria + a self-review — drafted by the AI in a single pass.
testStrategy.serviceHint: Pick an existing service or type a new one.
testStrategy.basisHint: Paste the test basis (endpoints, user stories, requirements) the strategy should cover.
testStrategy.basisPlaceholder: e.g. POST /policies — create a policy; GET /policies/{id} — fetch…
testStrategy.noStrategyYetBody: Generate one above; its versions and rationale appear here.
testStrategy.evidenceTooltip: Evidence why-doc: each section traced to its Jira/Confluence/code units

strategyDetail.savedSection: Saved "{{label}}" — a new version was created.
strategyDetail.sectionContentHint: Edit and save to add a new version to the history, or regenerate with the assistant.
strategyDetail.strategyApproved: Strategy approved — release plans will now pin to this version.
strategyDetail.lockedBanner: This version is <1>approved</1> and locked. Release plans for this service derive from it. To change it, generate a new version from the Test Strategy page.
strategyDetail.versionHistorySubtitle: Every edit or regeneration is saved as its own version — earlier versions are never overwritten.

testConditions.pageSubtitle_one: {{count}} condition · identified during test analysis (what to test)
testConditions.pageSubtitle_other: {{count}} conditions · identified during test analysis (what to test)
testConditions.splitNote: — decided per condition by risk × repeatability × stability.
testConditions.feedNote: Conditions marked <1>Automated</1> are included when tests are generated; Manual / Candidate feed <3>test-case creation</3>.
testConditions.cardSubtitle: Each condition traces a basis item and a risk forward to the cases that cover it.
testConditions.emptyState: No test conditions yet. Run test analysis for this service to generate them from the approved strategy.

multiSource.pageSubtitle: Build a test strategy from Jira + Confluence + code. Preview the clustered features, tidy them up, then generate.
multiSource.emptyPreviewTitle: No features found in those sources
multiSource.emptyPreviewBody: None of the selected sources returned anything to cluster. Widen the JQL, add a Confluence page, or point at a repo with code, then preview again.
multiSource.step1Subtitle: Any combination — code-only, Jira-only, pre-dev (Jira + Confluence), or all three.
multiSource.repoSlugHint: the repo's short name in its URL, e.g. ciam-policies
multiSource.epicKeyHint: fetch all child issues of this epic
multiSource.rootPageIdLabel: …or a parent page (includes everything under it)
multiSource.rootPageIdHint: include the whole page tree under this page
multiSource.hardFailTitle: A selected source returned no usable evidence
multiSource.hardFailFallback: Fix or deselect that source, then preview again.
multiSource.hardFailBlocked: Generation is blocked until this is resolved.
multiSource.carryForwardTitle: Some of your edits couldn't be carried forward
multiSource.step2Subtitle: Merge features that are the same capability, rename them, and pin the ones you've confirmed — then generate.
multiSource.coverageGapsSubtitle: Detected deterministically from the clustering — they update as you merge.
multiSource.estimatedCostNote: (rough — billed per feature when you generate).
multiSource.reextractTitle: Re-extract the same sources and carry your pins, renames and merges forward onto the fresh index
multiSource.footerNote: Preview runs the cheap stages only (extract + cluster); editing is free; the priced synthesis runs only when you click Generate.
multiSource.reextractedSomeNotCarried_one: Re-extracted — {{count}} edit couldn't be carried forward.
multiSource.reextractedSomeNotCarried_other: Re-extracted — {{count}} edits couldn't be carried forward.
multiSource.reextractedAllCarried: Re-extracted — your edits were carried forward.

testPlans.pageSubtitle: Release test plans with ISTQB risk analysis and a requirements-traceability matrix.
testPlans.newPlanSubtitle: Reconcile a release's requirements against existing tests; propose gap tests for approval.
testPlans.createGapTestsLabel: Create gap tests (still gated for approval)
testPlans.requiredFieldsToast: Service and fix version are required.
testPlans.emptyBody: Generate a release test plan above to see its deliverable and requirements-traceability matrix here.

testPlanDetail.subtitle: {{kind}} · {{status}} · est. {{cost}}
testPlanDetail.execJqlHint: Selects the tests to read execution status for. Prefilled from the fix version when set.
testPlanDetail.execEmpty: No execution data for this query yet.

testCases.pageSubtitle: Review, approve and push generated test cases to Xray.
testCases.generateSubtitle: ISTQB Test-Analyst cases from a basis (endpoints or stories) for the service above.
testCases.basisHint: What to design cases for — paste endpoints, acceptance criteria or stories.
testCases.basisPlaceholder: e.g. POST /policies — create; GET /policies/{id} — fetch; 404 when missing…
testCases.emptyLoadBody: Enter a service name above to review its test cases.
testCases.emptyNoCasesBody: Generate test cases for this service to review them here.

codegen.loadError: Couldn't load the generated test runs.
codegen.pageSubtitle: Advanced: template-driven generation from local filesystem paths. For the guided, git-native flow with a coverage plan and a Jira-linked PR, use Generate API Tests.
codegen.newGenerationSubtitle: Learn the template → analyze the service (AST) → generate tests → build-verify. The push & PR stay gated.
codegen.outputDirHint: Where the generated test repo is written.
codegen.serviceRepoHint: Local path to the service code to analyze.
codegen.templatePathHint: Leave blank to use the bundled BNC autotests template.
codegen.requiredFieldsToast: Service, service repo path and output directory are required.
codegen.noRunsBody: Trigger implement-tests for a service (via its workspace or the CLI) to review generated tests here.
codegen.selectRunBody: Pick a generation run to see its files and open a PR.
codegen.repoSlugHint: The repo where the PR will be opened.
codegen.overrideTitle: Build failed — override the no-PR-on-FAIL guard
codegen.generationCompleteToast: Generation complete — review the files below.

settings.pageSubtitle: Connect your tools and sign in to Copilot — everything Veritas needs, no terminal required.
settings.checklistSubtitle: What's configured and what still needs attention.
settings.engineSubtitle: Which engine runs the skills. Mock returns simulated results; switch to Copilot for real analysis.
settings.engineMockWarning: You're in mock mode — skill results are simulated and the Copilot sign-in below is not used. Switch to Copilot and restart to run real analysis.
settings.engineFieldHint: Takes effect after a restart (the gateway is wired at startup).
settings.enginePendingRestart: Pending: {{desired}} will be active after the next restart (currently running {{active}}).
settings.toastEngineRestart: Saved — restart Veritas to switch the engine.
settings.copilotSubtitle: The LLM behind Veritas. Sign in once with GitHub — the token is stored and refreshed automatically.
settings.workspaceHint: Your workspace name (in your URL: bitbucket.org/your-workspace).
settings.authOauth: OAuth (sign in via your identity provider)
settings.tokenHttpAccessTokenXray: HTTP access token (may reuse the Jira token)
settings.snykHint: Paste a personal API token from app.snyk.io → Account settings. Veritas reads your Snyk organizations (app-ids) with it — read-only.
settings.noteBitbucketServerDc: Server/DC: BEARER = an HTTP access token (PAT); BASIC = your BNC username + password. No workspace — the project key (app-id) is entered on the Validate screen.
settings.hintAlreadySetUsername: Already set — leave blank to keep it
settings.hintAlreadyConfigured: Already configured — leave blank to keep it

genApi.noReposFound: No repos found for that app.
genApi.testsGenerated: Tests generated — review them, then open a PR.
genApi.submittedForApproval: Submitted for approval — approve it on the Gates page to open the PR.
genApi.pageSubtitle: Pick a service from git, see what's covered, and choose what to generate. Nothing is written without your approval.
genApi.step1Title: Which service do you want tests for?
genApi.step1Subtitle: Pick the service repo from git — we only read it.
genApi.gitAppHint: Your Bitbucket app id, e.g. APP7571.
genApi.branchHintDefault: Leave on the default branch unless you need another.
genApi.step2Subtitle: If you already have a test repo we'll update it; otherwise we start fresh.
genApi.testRepoHint: Leave on “create them” to generate from scratch.
genApi.jiraTicketHint: The work item these tests commit under — its key goes in the branch, commit and PR so Jira links them.
genApi.jiraSearchPlaceholder: Search Jira, or paste a key / URL (e.g. CIAM-1842)
genApi.jiraSearchError: Couldn't reach Jira to search. Check the connection and try again.
genApi.step3SubtitleRefactor: Scanned {{count}} existing test files — we'll update, never replace.
genApi.step3SubtitleScratch: No tests yet — we'll create a fresh set.
genApi.reconciling: Reconciling the API against your tests…
genApi.uncheckHint: Uncheck anything you don't want. By default we generate the gaps.
genApi.everythingCovered: Everything is covered — nothing to generate.
genApi.nextTokensPrefix: Next we'll set up tokens, then write the tests to
genApi.nextTokensSuffix: on a branch — nothing is pushed until you click “Open pull request”.
genApi.step4Title: How many tokens does this service need?
genApi.step4Subtitle: One per API group. Each gets an Okta token by private-key JWT — we store only the URL, client id and scopes; your private key stays in oktaCredentials.json.
genApi.publicServiceNote: Public service — every endpoint is called without a token.
genApi.privateKeyNote3: value — it never leaves your environment.
genApi.step5Subtitle: Generated tests for {{name}} — review, then open a PR when you're ready.
genApi.buildSkippedNote: These tests weren't compiled here (build skipped) — don't assume they compile; CI will verify before merge.
genApi.testItLocallyBody: Veritas generated the tests and opened the PR. Running them against the live API is done from your machine (or CI) — pull the branch and run your suite:
genApi.openAnywayTitle: Build failed — open the PR anyway (override)
genApi.openingPrNote: Opening a PR pushes a branch for review — it never merges on its own.
genApi.tokenForEndpoints: for the endpoints under its path prefix
genApi.privateKeyFieldHint: Veritas never sees the key — it's a $sensitive value.

engine.seededTooltip: This environment runs on a seeded demo portfolio — numbers are illustrative, the product is real.
engine.mockTooltip: The active engine is the mock — results are simulated, not real analysis. Switch engines in Settings.

copilot.expired: The code expired — close and try again.
copilot.failed: Sign-in failed — close and try again.

activity.title: Activity
activity.empty: Nothing needs your attention right now.
activity.pageSubtitle: Everything running, waiting on you, or finished in the last 7 days.
activity.emptyBody: Nothing is running right now, and nothing finished in the last 7 days. When you start a check, generate tests or apply a security fix, it will show up here — live.
